📊
Pitch Deck
Click on any heading below to expand and view details
🎓
Eligibility Criteria
▼
  • Students registered for Intermediate or Final Course undergoing articleship training.
  • Students who are members of CA Students Association are not eligible to participate.
  • Students who have completed their Articleship are not eligible to participate.
  • Students will be allowed to participate in any one of the competitions announced in a year.
  • Winners of previous year of National Level Competition cannot participate in the same competition in the current year.
📝
Topics
▼
  • Startup idea in any sector (FinTech, AgriTech, HealthTech, EdTech, etc.)
  • Social enterprise solving a real-world problem
  • Sustainable business model with financial projections
  • Technology-driven solution for CA profession
📋
Rules & Instructions
▼
  • Participants will need to submit their pitch deck by a specified deadline.
  • The pitch deck must follow the specified format (as per Annexure A) and include key elements such as the problem being solved, the solution, market opportunity, revenue model, and competition.
  • The branch-level competition will be judged by a panel of judges appointed by the branch.
  • First Winning Team at the Branch-level competition will qualify for the regional-level competition.
  • Each team will be given 15 mins time to present their idea, followed by 5 mins of Q&A round.
  • Note: Students should submit their mobile phones, smartwatch, earphones, or any other gadget which can be misused during the competitions
⚖️
Judging Criteria
▼
  • Problem and Solution: The problem and solution being addressed should be clearly identified and explained. The solution should be innovative, feasible, and scalable. – 20 marks
  • Market Opportunity: The market opportunity should be clearly defined and should show a potential for growth. – 10 marks
  • Revenue Model: The revenue model should be clearly explained and should show a potential for generating revenue. – 10 marks
  • Competition: The competition should be clearly identified, and the solution should show how it is better than or different from existing solutions. – 10 marks
  • Presentation: The presentation should be engaging, clear, and concise. The pitch deck should be well-designed and easy to follow. – 15 marks
  • Q&A: The participant's ability to answer questions from the judges should be evaluated. The participant should be able to demonstrate a good understanding of their solution, market, and competition. - 15 Marks
  • Overall Impact: The overall impact of the pitch deck should be evaluated. The solution should show potential for creating value, making an impact, and addressing a significant problem. - 20 Marks
🏆
Winner Count
▼
  • 1st Prize – Gold Medal + Certificate + Cash Prize + Mentorship opportunity
  • 2nd Prize – Silver Medal + Certificate + Cash Prize
  • 3rd Prize – Bronze Medal + Certificate
📌
Format for the Pitch Deck
▼
  • Slide 1: Cover Slide
  • o Title of Pitch Deck Competition
  • o Name of Participant(s)
  • o Name of Branch/Region/National Competition
  • Slide 2: Problem Statement
  • o Identify the problem being solved
  • o Explain why it is important
  • o Provide statistics or evidence to support the problem statement
  • Slide 3: Solution
  • o Explain the solution being proposed
  • o Show how the solution addresses the problem
  • o Highlight the unique selling proposition (USP) of the solution
  • Slide 4: Market Opportunity
  • o Define the target market for the solution
  • o Estimate the size of the market opportunity
  • o Explain why the solution is needed in the market
  • Slide 5: Revenue Model
  • o Explain the revenue model for the solution
  • o Show how the solution will generate revenue
  • o Highlight the potential for scalability and profitability
  • Slide 6: Competition
  • o Identify the competition in the market
  • o Explain how the solution is better or different from existing solutions
  • o Highlight any competitive advantages or barriers to entry
  • o Key differentiator from the Competition
  • Slide 7: Team
  • o Introduce the team behind the solution
  • o Highlight the team's qualifications, skills, and experience
  • o Explain why the team is capable of executing the solution
  • Slide 8: Milestones
  • o Highlight the key milestones for the solution
  • o Show how the solution will progress over time
  • o Include any achievements or milestones achieved so far
  • Slide 9: Financial Projections
  • o Provide financial projections for the solution
  • o Include revenue, costs, profits, and cash flow
  • o Highlight the potential for growth and scalability
  • Slide 10: Call to Action
  • o Summarize the key points of the pitch deck
  • o Call to action for investors or supporters to take action
